title: Falls Screening for PE-Backed Home Care

seo.title: Falls Screening for PE-Backed Home Care | Able Care
seo.description: Standardized falls screening across every franchise branch. Protect HHVBP. Defend enterprise value. Under five minutes per client, non-clinical staff.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Older woman using GripAble sensor at home during care visit
blocks.headline: One screening workflow, every branch.
blocks.subtitle: For PE-backed and franchised home care operators who need standardization, HHVBP protection and a low-training rollout. Four objective metrics in under five minutes.

blocks.heading: The franchise standardization problem
blocks.body:
Your branches run different screening processes, if they screen at all. Staff turnover means your best-trained people leave every six months. HHVBP is tying reimbursement to falls outcomes, and your data trail is inconsistent at best. Meanwhile, corporate needs to demonstrate ROI to investors and show a scalable care quality story across the portfolio.

Able Assess solves this with one standardized protocol that any staff member (clinical or non-clinical) can deliver in under five minutes. Same workflow in every branch. Same data format for corporate reporting. Same defensible evidence trail for HHVBP.

blocks.heading: How it works across your franchise network

steps.title: Deploy
steps.description: Ship sensors to every branch. Staff are proficient in under 15 minutes. No clinical certification required.

steps.title: Screen
steps.description: Every client gets the same four-metric assessment: grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed, timed up-and-go. Under five minutes per client.

steps.title: Stratify
steps.description: Instant risk banding flags at-risk clients. Care managers get objective data to justify level-of-care decisions to payers and families.

steps.title: Report
steps.description: Aggregate data across branches for corporate-level reporting. Track HHVBP performance, demonstrate outcomes to investors, and identify underperforming sites.

primaryCta.label: See the full Able Assess workflow

metrics.name: Grip Strength
metrics.whyItMatters: Predicts hospitalization risk. Justifies level-of-care coding under Section GG and OASIS.

metrics.name: Sit-to-Stand
metrics.whatItMeasures: Lower limb strength and transfer ability
metrics.whyItMatters: Identifies clients at risk of falls during ADLs. Key HHVBP measure.

metrics.name: Gait Speed
metrics.whyItMatters: Flags clients whose mobility is declining before a fall event.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.whyItMatters: Standard clinical fall risk indicator. Captures the full picture alongside grip strength.

blocks.heading: Model the HHVBP impact across your branches
blocks.promoBody: One prevented hospitalization per branch per quarter pays for the program. See the numbers for your network size.

testimonials.quote: We caught decline six weeks earlier than we would have with our old paper process. The data gave us something to show the family and the payer.
testimonials.attribution: Clinical Director, US home care agency

testimonials.quote: It paid for itself in the first quarter. The real win was the consistency: every branch running the same protocol.
testimonials.attribution: Operations Lead, PE-backed home care franchise

blocks.heading: Built for non-clinical staff
blocks.body: Your caregivers are not clinicians, and they do not need to be. Able Assess was designed so any staff member can deliver a clinical-grade screening with zero training overhead. The sensor guides them through every step. Results are instant. No interpretation required.
blocks.imageAlt: Care manager and client using GripAble sensor and tablet app

items.question: How quickly can we roll out across 50+ branches?
items.answer: Most franchise operators deploy across their full network in 30 days. We ship sensors direct to each branch, and staff are proficient in under 15 minutes with the guided workflow. No site visits required for initial deployment.

items.question: Can non-clinical staff actually run this?
items.answer: Yes. Able Assess was built specifically for non-clinical caregivers. The sensor and app guide each step. Staff do not need to interpret results. The system scores and risk-bands automatically. This is critical for franchises with high turnover and 1099 contract staff.

items.question: How does this protect our HHVBP performance?
items.answer: Falls outcomes directly feed HHVBP measures. By screening every client at intake and tracking longitudinally, you create a defensible data trail that shows proactive falls prevention. The objective data also justifies level-of-care decisions to payers.

items.question: What is the ROI for a typical franchise?
items.answer: One prevented hospitalization per branch per quarter typically covers the program cost. Use our ROI calculator to model the impact for your specific network size and client volume.

items.question: Does it integrate with our existing systems?
items.answer: Able Assess integrates with major EHR platforms and supports OASIS data export. The portal provides corporate-level dashboards without requiring individual branch system changes.

items.question: What hardware do we need?
items.answer: One GripAble sensor per branch (or per caregiver, for larger branches) and a tablet running the Able Assess app. The sensor is drop-proof, auto-calibrating, and FDA listed.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in a franchise home care workflow
blocks.body: 30 minutes with the clinical team. Tailored to multi-site home care operations. Focused on your workflow.
